channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Inquest hears NDIS provider delayed suicide threat reports before teen's death
item.description: The inquest in Southport is examining whether any deficiencies in the boy's care or accommodation caused or contributed to his death.
item.dc:creator: Charmayne Allison

media:group.media:description: Quinn Cook died by suicide in 2023.

item.title: Man critically injured following Queensland vehicle and pedestrian incident
item.description: Police have declared multiple crime scenes in a Queensland town after a man was critically injured this afternoon.
item.dc:creator: James C Taylor

media:group.media:description: Police have declared three crime scenes in Childers, 300km north of Brisbane.

item.title: Whyalla locals react to news of steelworks' final bidders
item.description: Whyalla locals welcome more certainty about the future ownership of the city's steelworks, but say it is still not enough to warrant business investment and workers coming back to the Spencer Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Eugene Boisvert, Declan Durrant, and James Wakelin

media:group.media:description: John O'Halloran says he hopes to see "some finality" around the future of Whyalla steelworks.

item.title: NT child protection bill one year in the making had 'limited' consultation
item.description: Despite taking a year to prepare a controversial bill surrounding child protection, the NT Department of Children and Families says consultation with organisations was "limited".
item.dc:creator: Jack Hislop

media:group.media:description: Brent Warren says "time constraints" meant there was little opportunity to consult on changes to child protection legislation.

item.title: Live: Hotly anticipated State of Origin series opener to kick off in Sydney
item.description: New South Wales hosts the State of Origin opener in Sydney as Queensland looks for an ambush in enemy territory. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale, Jon Healy, Luke Pentony, and Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: The State of Origin Shield is in Queensland's hands.

item.title: Bulk carrier rescues two fishermen and Smokey the dog after boat sinks
item.description: An emergency distress beacon activation off the North Queensland coast yesterday afternoon sparked a coordinated rescue involving a bulk carrier and rescue jet.
item.dc:creator: Liam O'Connell and Yasmine Wright Gittins

media:group.media:description: The Challenger jet located the two fishermen and their dog in a lifeboat.

item.title: Labor MP defends electoral registration to empty block amid rebuild
item.description: Dickson MP Ali France has been referred to the Australian Electoral Commission with claims she is registered to vote at a vacant suburban block in her north Brisbane electorate.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas McElroy

media:group.media:description: Ali France says she is rebuilding her home.

item.title: Trainee mechanics worked on hired EVs without Hertz's knowledge, ACT assembly hears
item.description: The professional misconduct claims were levelled at Australia's first TAFE Electric Vehicle Centre of Excellence.
item.dc:creator: Adam Shirley

media:group.media:description: Hire car company Hertz offers electric vehicles in its collection.

item.title: Brother of hit-run victim says family will appeal NT court's 'light' sentence
item.description: The family of a man who was left to die alongside his severely injured brother and father after a hit-and-run crash, say they will appeal the "light" sentence handed to the man who was behind the wheel.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Barrett Peters

media:group.media:description: The NT Supreme Court sentenced Christian Mpongo to three years' jail on Wednesday.

item.title: Rodent faeces, animal blood, mould spark $60k penalty for country bakery
item.description: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread and a jar of rodent faeces at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.
item.dc:creator: Rosemary Murphy

media:group.media:description: The City of Albany prosecuted 21 charges against the business and its owners.

item.title: Maugean skates released after more than two years in captivity
item.description: A pair of endangered rays taken from a Tasmanian harbour as a species "insurance policy" have been released back into the wild after helping to produce more than 400 eggs for a captive research program.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Heard

media:group.media:description: The female skate had produced about 80 hatchlings for the captive program being run by IMAS.

item.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight
item.description: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of yesterday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.
item.dc:creator: Sara Tomevska

media:group.media:description: A woman barred from Australia attempted to board the flight that touched down in Melbourne yesterday.

item.title: Copi Mineral Sands Project near Victoria-NSW border gets green light
item.description: A mineral sands project of global strategic importance receives provisional approval from the New South Wales government.
item.dc:creator: Wade Stephens and Andrew Schmidt

media:group.media:description: RZ Resources' Copi Mine project has been approved by the NSW planning department.

item.title: Feds warn NT's law-and-order agenda could breach human rights
item.description: Federal Attorney-General Michelle Rowland warns her Northern Territory counterpart that the NT's tough-on-crime agenda could breach Australia's international human rights obligations.
item.dc:creator: Jason Walls and Olivana Lathouris

media:group.media:description: Michelle Rowland says "no child should face charges or court processes without legal representation".

item.title: Bondi shooting victim labelled 'subhuman' in online abuse of inquiry witnesses
item.description: Victims of antisemitism have been targeted with death threats and depicted as animals in AI-generated images after giving evidence to the royal commission examining the Bondi terror attack.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: The royal commission is looking at the rise of antisemitism in Australia and the events leading up to the Bondi terror attack.

item.title: No 'plan B' for submarines if AUKUS fails, says defence minister
item.description: The defence minister brushes off calls for a "plan B" in case AUKUS fails, declaring it would be a step towards "giving up" on acquiring any new submarines at all.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Concerns are growing about the AUKUS deal to deliver Australian nuclear submarine capability.

item.title: Record-breaking rain welcomed in drought-hit parts of NSW
item.description: Up to 100 millimetres of rain has been welcomed across drought-hit parts of western NSW, but some farmers expecting relief were left disappointed.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Bohane, Amelia Bernasconi, and Lucy Thackray

media:group.media:description: The Tourle family managed to sow their crop just in time before 100mm fell at their Toongi property.

item.title: Accused 'money mules' refused bail after allegedly moving millions from scams
item.description: Police say so-called "money mules" facilitate the swift movement of fraudulently obtained money.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: Police allege they found a large amount of cash when searching the home of Motasem Yousef Abed Abu-Hamad.

item.title: Kalshi and Polymarket are billion dollar bets banned from Australia — for now
item.description: Kalshi and Polymarket have gone from obscure start-ups to two of the world's biggest prediction markets where users bet on yes-or-no questions spanning politics, pop culture and war.
item.dc:creator: Myles Houlbrook-Walk

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump Jr is an advisor to the two biggest prediction market platforms, Kalshi and Polymarket.

item.title: Man to stand trial for alleged murder of woman found in Cobram home
item.description: A man charged over the murder of a woman who was found dead in a Northern Victorian home has been committed to stand trial over her death.
item.dc:creator: Charmaine Manuel

media:group.media:description: Prem Kumar pleaded not guilty to murder.

item.title: Chalmers flags 'legitimate' small business CGT debate
item.description: The government is treading carefully on carve-outs to its capital gains tax legislation amid backlash to its plan.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has faced ongoing questions about capital gains tax carve-outs.

item.title: Pentagon denies reports of clash with SpaceX over Starlink price hike
item.description: The Pentagon has denied claims that the department has been clashing with Elon Musk's SpaceX over costs to use its satellite internet network, which the military's kamikaze drones rely upon in the war against Iran.

media:group.media:description: Reuters has reported the Pentagon clashed with Elon Musk's SpaceX over hiked-up costs to use its satellite internet network in the first few weeks of the Iran war.

item.title: Bystanders foil alleged kidnap attempt of woman changing tyre in Sydney
item.description: A 27-year-old woman was changing a tyre when a man allegedly threatened her with a knife and attempted to drag her into an SUV, NSW Police said.

media:group.media:description: Two men have been charged over their alleged role in an attempted kidnapping of a woman at knife-point in Sydney's south-west.

item.title: Teaching student charged with online child abuse offences
item.description: A Victorian teaching student, who had recently worked as a childcare centre cook, a foster carer and was a school council president, is facing charges relating to online child abuse material.
item.dc:creator: Anna McGuinness

media:group.media:description: Daniel Grey has been charged with online child abuse material-related offences.

item.title: Council buys land for $1, but what's the catch?
item.description: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.
item.dc:creator: Piper Duffy

media:group.media:description: The vacant block of land is just south of Geraldton's CBD.
